title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) with our easy, step-by-step recipe. A comforting and flavorful Turkish classic!

Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm bowl of homemade Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a comforting and flavorful dish perfect for any meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its velvety smooth texture and comforting flavors. This hearty and nutritious soup is a staple in Turkish households, often enjoyed as a starter or a light meal. It's incredibly easy to prepare, offering a taste of authentic Turkish cuisine in every spoonful.

ingredients.notes: for the topping, or to taste

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables and Sauté
instructions.text: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion, carrot, and diced potato. Sauté for about 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils and tomato paste to the pot. Stir well and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing the tomato paste to deepen in color and flavor.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup Base
instructions.text: Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Add salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and mushy.

instructions.title: Blend to a Smooth Consistency
instructions.text: Once the lentils and vegetables are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili Butter Topping
instructions.text: While the soup is blending, prepare the traditional topping. In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted and slightly foamy, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the aromatic chili butter topping over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which adds a wonderful brightness to the soup.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, some Turkish chefs add a pinch of cumin along with the tomato paste when sautéing the vegetables.
If you prefer a thicker soup, you can add a tablespoon of flour when sautéing the vegetables, cooking it for a minute before adding the tomato paste.
Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article:
Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup, is more than just a dish; it’s a warm embrace in a bowl, a staple of Turkish hospitality, and a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage. From bustling Istanbul street corners to cozy family kitchens, this vibrant, creamy, and utterly comforting soup holds a special place in the hearts of Turks and food enthusiasts worldwide. Its beautiful reddish-orange hue, silky texture, and delicate balance of savory and tangy flavors make it an irresistible starter, a light lunch, or a soothing breakfast. If you're looking to explore the depth of Turkish cuisine, Mercimek Corbasi is the perfect, delicious starting point.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi translates directly to "lentil soup," but it’s specifically known for its use of red lentils, which cook down to create an incredibly smooth and creamy consistency. This traditional Turkish soup is characterized by its simplicity and profound flavor. At its core, it’s made from red lentils, often simmered with aromatics like onion and garlic, a touch of tomato paste for depth and color, and seasoned with a blend of spices. The magic truly happens when it's puréed to a velvety texture and then often finished with a drizzle of "nane yağı" – a vibrant, sizzling butter infused with dried mint and sometimes red pepper flakes.

Historically, lentils have been a cornerstone of the Anatolian diet, providing an affordable and nutritious source of protein and fiber. Mercimek Corbasi embodies this tradition, offering a wholesome, nourishing meal that is both delicious and incredibly satisfying. It's naturally gluten-free (if made without flour thickeners, which is typical) and easily adaptable for vegetarian and vegan diets, making it a universally appealing dish. Whether served piping hot on a cold winter's day or as a light, refreshing starter in warmer months, this soup is a true culinary delight that showcases the heartwarming essence of Turkish cooking.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Creating an authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup requires a handful of simple, readily available ingredients. The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in how these basic components transform into something truly extraordinary.

Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show! These quick-cooking lentils break down easily, giving the soup its signature creamy texture and beautiful color. Ensure you rinse them thoroughly before cooking.
*   **Onion:** Finely chopped, it forms the aromatic base, adding sweetness and depth to the soup.
*   **Garlic:** Crushed or minced, garlic complements the onion and provides a pungent, savory kick.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A crucial ingredient for Turkish cuisine, tomato paste adds a concentrated tomato flavor and a rich, appealing color. A good quality Turkish tomato paste makes a difference.
*   **Butter or Olive Oil:** For sautéing the aromatics and creating the flavorful base. Butter adds a richer taste, while olive oil keeps it vegan.
*   **Vegetable or Chicken Broth/Water:** The liquid base for the soup. Broth adds more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially if you plan to enhance it with spices.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** Essential for the finishing garnish, dried mint adds a wonderfully fresh, herbaceous aroma and flavor that is characteristic of Mercimek Corbasi.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber) or Paprika:** For a gentle warmth and color in the soup, and optionally for the spicy butter garnish. Use mild or hot, depending on your preference.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper:** To season the soup to perfection.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving, a squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens the flavors and is non-negotiable for an authentic experience.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Mercimek Corbasi

Making this delicious Turkish Red Lentil Soup at home is surprisingly simple. Follow these steps for a comforting bowl of Mercimek Corbasi:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup (about 200g) of red lentils under cold running water in a fine-mesh sieve until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, preventing the soup from becoming too frothy.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter or heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in 1 tablespoon of good quality tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly. This toasts the tomato paste, deepening its flavor and color. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and ½ teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or paprika) and cook for 30 seconds more, until fragrant.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in 6 cups (about 1.5 liters) of vegetable or chicken broth (or water). Add 1 teaspoon of salt and ½ teaspoon of black pepper.
5.  **Simmer:** Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend Until Smooth:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be cautious with hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
7.  **Prepare the Mint Butter (Nane Yağı - Optional but Recommended):** In a small saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, remove from heat and stir in 1-2 teaspoons of dried mint and a pinch of red pepper flakes (pul biber). The mint will sizzle briefly and become fragrant.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the prepared mint butter. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and crusty bread.

## Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Mercimek Corbasi at home is easier than you think with a few expert tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This is crucial. Rinsing removes starch and any impurities, leading to a cleaner flavor and preventing the soup from becoming gummy.
*   **Toast the Tomato Paste:** Don't just dump it in. Sautéing the tomato paste for a minute or two before adding the liquid caramelizes its sugars, intensifying its flavor and deepening the soup's color.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Butter Garnish:** The "nane yağı" (mint-infused butter) is not just a garnish; it's an integral part of the Mercimek Corbasi experience. The sizzling butter with dried mint and red pepper flakes adds a layer of richness, aroma, and visual appeal that truly elevates the soup.
*   **Blend Until Velvety Smooth:** For that classic creamy texture, blend the soup until there are no discernible lentil chunks. An immersion blender makes this incredibly easy, but a regular blender works too (just be careful with hot liquids).
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils absorb a lot of liquid. If your soup is too thick after blending, simply whisk in a little more hot water or broth until you reach your desired consistency. If it's too thin, simmer it gently for a few more minutes to reduce.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. A squeeze of lemon juice right before eating brightens all the flavors, adding a necessary tangy counterpoint to the richness of the lentils and butter.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways throughout the day.

*   **Traditional Starter:** In Turkey, it's most commonly served as a starter to almost any meal. Its light yet satisfying nature perfectly prepares the palate for subsequent dishes.
*   **Light Lunch or Dinner:** Paired with a generous squeeze of lemon and a basket of fresh, crusty bread (like Turkish pide or simit), Mercimek Corbasi makes for a wonderfully fulfilling and healthy light meal.
*   **Breakfast Staple:** Believe it or not, many Turks enjoy this soup for breakfast, especially during colder months. It’s a warm, nourishing way to start the day.
*   **Accompaniments:**
    *   **Lemon Wedges:** Absolutely essential! The acidity cuts through the richness and brightens the flavors.
    *   **Crusty Bread:** Essential for dipping and soaking up every last drop.
    *   **Pickles (Turşu):** A common pairing in Turkey, the tanginess of pickles offers a delightful contrast.
    *   **Simple Salad:** A fresh green salad with a light vinaigrette can complement the soup beautifully.
    *   **Extra Chili Flakes:** For those who like more heat, a sprinkle of extra pul biber (Turkish red pepper flakes) can be added at the table.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** The basic recipe is already vegetarian. To make it fully vegan, simply use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and for the garnish, and ensure you use vegetable broth.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** For extra nutrition and flavor, you can add finely diced carrots, potatoes, or celery along with the onions and garlic. These will cook down with the lentils and contribute to the soup's body and sweetness.
*   **Spice Profile:** While dried mint and red pepper flakes are traditional, you can also add a pinch of cumin for an earthy note, or a touch of smoked paprika for a deeper flavor. Aleppo pepper (pul biber) is also a fantastic alternative to regular red pepper flakes, offering a milder heat and fruitier undertones.
*   **Herb Freshness:** While dried mint is traditional for the garnish, you can stir in fresh chopped parsley or cilantro at the very end for a burst of freshness in the soup itself.
*   **Thickening Agents (Optional):** Some recipes may include a small amount of flour (1-2 tablespoons) sautéed with the tomato paste to thicken the soup, though the red lentils usually provide enough body on their own. If using, make sure to cook out the raw flour taste.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Mercimek Corbasi spicy?**
A: Traditionally, Mercimek Corbasi is not inherently spicy. Any heat usually comes from the red pepper flakes used in the mint-infused butter garnish, which can be adjusted to your preference (mild or hot). The soup itself is generally mild and comforting.

**Q: Can I make Turkish Red Lentil Soup ahead of time?**
A: Absolutely! Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a little water or broth if it has thickened too much.

**Q: Can I freeze Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: Yes, this soup freezes beautifully. Allow it to cool completely, then transfer it to freezer-safe containers or bags. It can be frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat on the stovetop.

**Q: What kind of lentils should I use for Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: Red lentils are essential for authentic Mercimek Corbasi. They break down easily and create the characteristic creamy, smooth texture and vibrant color that the soup is known for. Other lentil varieties will yield a different texture and appearance.

**Q: Is Mercimek Corbasi healthy?**
A: Yes, it is very healthy! Red lentils are an excellent source of plant-based protein, fiber, iron, and other essential nutrients. The soup is naturally low in fat (especially if using olive oil) and calories, making it a nutritious and fulfilling meal option.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey. Its simple ingredients belie a depth of flavor that is both comforting and invigorating. Whether you're seeking a nutritious meal, a warming starter, or simply a taste of authentic Turkish cuisine, this Turkish Red Lentil Soup is a recipe you'll return to again and again. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be enchanted by the magic of Mercimek Corbasi. Afiyet Olsun (Enjoy your meal)!
